license: MIT

# MSBuild Item Management Patterns

Canonical patterns for working with item groups, from `Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets`.

## Include / Remove / Update — Three Operations

| Operation | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `Include` | Add new items to the group | Creating items with identity + metadata |
| `Remove` | Remove items matching a pattern | Excluding files or clearing a group |
| `Update` | Modify metadata on existing items | Adding/changing metadata without re-adding |

### Include — Add Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Generated\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Remove — Subtract Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Remove specific items -->
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />

  <!-- Set subtraction: prior minus current -->
  <_CleanOrphanFileWrites Include="@(_CleanPriorFileWrites)"
      Exclude="@(_CleanCurrentFileWrites)" />

  <!-- Clear an entire group -->
  <_Temporary Remove="@(_Temporary)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Update — Modify Existing Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="@(EmbeddedResource)"
      Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections'">
    <GenerateSource>true</GenerateSource>
    <ClassName>Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections.SR</ClassName>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

`Update` does not add items — it only modifies items already in the group.

## Item Batching — %(Metadata)

When `%(Metadata)` appears in target attributes or task parameters, MSBuild **batches** execution per unique metadata value.

### Target-level batching (Outputs)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateSatelliteAssemblies"
    Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(_SatelliteAssemblyResourceInputs)"
    Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Culture)\$(TargetName).resources.dll">
  <!-- Runs once per unique Culture value -->
</Target>
```

### Task-level batching

```xml
<Copy SourceFiles="@(_SourceItems)"
    DestinationFiles="@(_SourceItems->'$(OutDir)%(TargetPath)')">
</Copy>
```

### Per-item filtering with Condition

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <_ResxOutput Include="@(EmbeddedResource->'%(OutputResource)')"
      Condition="'%(EmbeddedResource.WithCulture)' == 'false'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Batching rules

- `%(Metadata)` in `Condition` or `Outputs` → target batches per unique value.
- `%(Metadata)` in task parameters → task batches per unique value.
- **Do not mix `%()` from different item groups** in the same expression — this causes a cross-product (see Common Pitfalls).

## Item Transforms — @(Item->'expression')

Transforms create new item lists by applying an expression to each item:

```xml
<!-- Transform file paths to destinations -->
<Copy SourceFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly)"
    DestinationFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly->'$(OutDir)%(Filename)%(Extension)')"/>

<!-- Transform with separator for display -->
<Message Text="Files: @(Compile->'%(Filename)', ', ')" />
```

## Exclude Pattern — Set Subtraction on Include

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="**\*.cs" Exclude="Generated\**;Tests\**" />
</ItemGroup>
```

`Exclude` only works on `Include` — it cannot be used with `Update` or `Remove`.

## Conditional Item Inclusion

```xml
<!-- Condition on ItemGroup — all or nothing -->
<ItemGroup Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'">
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines" />
</ItemGroup>

<!-- Condition on individual items -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines"
      Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## PrivateAssets on Tool/Analyzer Packages

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
  <PackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Common Pitfalls

### Cross-product batching

Referencing `%(Metadata)` from two different item groups creates O(N×M) executions:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Cross-product of @(Source) × @(Config) -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with %(Config.Identity)" />

<!-- GOOD: Reference one group via batching, the other via property -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with $(ConfigFile)" />
```

### Generated files in source tree

Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (obj/), not the source directory. Source-tree generation pollutes version control and can cause duplicate compilation via globs.

### Missing FileWrites

Every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)` for `dotnet clean` support.

Let me analyze the csproj file against the skill context to identify all the issues.

Looking at the project file, I can see several problems:

1. **Cross-product batching (line 53-54)**: `ValidateGeneratedCode` target references `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` in the same Message task — two different item groups batched together = 3×2 = 6 executions instead of the expected sequential validation.

2. **CS2002 duplicate source files (line 16-18)**: SDK-style projects auto-glob `**/*.cs` including `**/*.g.cs`. Then line 16 does `<Compile Include="**\*.g.cs">` which adds them again. 

3. **FileWrites in static ItemGroup (line 42)**: `@(ProtoFile->'...')` in a static ItemGroup evaluates at evaluation time, before `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` may be fully resolved. More critically, FileWrites should be populated inside the target that creates the files, not in a static item group — the `_CleanGetCurrentAndPriorFileWrites` target needs to read from the `.filewriteslist` file during target execution, so static FileWrites won't work correctly.

Actually, reconsidering this — the skill context says every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)`. A static ItemGroup would register them at evaluation time regardless of whether GenerateProtoCode ran, which means they'd be listed even if the target was skipped in an incremental build. The real issue is whether the static ItemGroup approach aligns with the conventional pattern of adding FileWrites inside the target itself.

Now I'm looking at the duplicate Compile items problem. The SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs` files, but line 16 explicitly includes `**/*.g.cs` again, and then line 36 in the target adds them a third time. The fix would be to use `Update` instead of `Include` on line 16 to set metadata, and remove the `Include` from inside the target since they're already globbed. But wait — the generated files go to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (the obj/ folder), while the SDK glob covers the project directory. So line 16 would only match existing `.g.cs` files in the root, not the ones being generated in obj/. The target on line 36 adds those obj/ ones, so line 16 might be duplicating any pre-existing generated files in the project directory.

The cross-product batching issue is on lines 53-54, where `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` are used together in the same task. The FileWrites in the static ItemGroup should be moved inside the target instead. And the Clean target doesn't actually delete the generated files from disk — it just removes them from the Compile item, so it's incomplete.

Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify all the issues and fix them:

**Issue 1: CS2002 — Duplicate Compile items (line 16-18)**
SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs` which picks up any `.g.cs` files in the project tree. Line 16 does `<Compile Include="**\*.g.cs">` which adds them again → CS2002 warning. Fix: Use `<Compile Update="**\*.g.cs">` to set metadata on already-globbed items without re-adding.

**Issue 2: Cross-product batching (line 53-54)**


The `ValidateGeneratedCode` task is batching on both `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` simultaneously, which creates a cross-product of 3×2=6 executions. What's needed is per-ProtoFile validation where each file gets checked against all rules. I should batch only on `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and pass `@(ValidationRule)` as a complete list to each task invocation.

**Issue 3: FileWrites in static ItemGroup (line 42-43)**
`FileWrites` is declared in a static ItemGroup outside any target, so it gets evaluated at project load time. It needs to be populated dynamically during the build, so it should be moved into a target where it can capture the actual outputs.

**Issue 4: CleanGeneratedCode target (line 58-62)**
This target removes `**\*.g.cs` from the Compile item group but doesn't actually delete files from disk. If `FileWrites` is properly registered inside the target that generates files, the built-in Clean target will handle deletion automatically. The current approach is also too broad — it removes all `.g.cs` files rather than just the ones this project generated. The user's complaint about clean removing too many files might stem from the static `FileWrites` registration adding entries even when code generation didn't run, or from the overly broad removal pattern affecting unrelated generated files.

The real problem is that generated files go to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (the obj/ directory), which the SDK glob excludes by default. The explicit `<Compile Include="**\*.g.cs">` on line 16 would only match `.g.cs` files in the source tree, not in obj/, so the target's removal logic might be targeting the wrong files entirely.

There's also a latent bug: if someone places a `.g.cs` file in the project directory, it would get double-included since the pattern should use `Update` instead of `Include`. And the `<FileWrites>` tracking for cleanup isn't properly set up, which means the Clean target won't actually delete the generated files from disk. So the real problem is that static FileWrites get evaluated once at evaluation time, but if the build is incremental and GenerateProtoCode gets skipped, those FileWrites are still registered and written to the `.filewriteslist` file even though the files were never actually generated. This creates a mismatch between what's tracked for cleanup and what was actually produced. The real problem is that on incremental builds, when the target gets skipped, those generated files never make it into `@(Compile)` — and the static glob pattern on line 16 won't catch files in the obj/ directory. So the generated code disappears from compilation on subsequent builds.

I'm seeing two main issues to fix: the duplicate compile entries from the glob pattern re-adding SDK-globbed files, and the incremental build problem where generated files in obj/ need to persist in the compilation list across builds.

The cross-product batching is also problematic—running the generation task six times when it should only run once per proto file. I need to batch only on ProtoFile and pass the validation rules as a transform instead. The FileWrites tracking should move into the GenerateProtoCode target itself for proper cleanup, and the CleanGeneratedCode target becomes redundant once file tracking is correct.

I'm realizing there's a potential conflict here—both the static ItemGroup and the target's dynamic Compile Include could try to add the same generated file on the first build, so I need to reconsider the approach.

On first build, the generated files don't exist yet, so a static Compile Include with an `Exists` condition won't match, and the target will run to create them. On subsequent builds, the target is skipped but the static ItemGroup picks up the now-existing files. The real issue is whether the `Condition` on items with transforms behaves as expected in MSBuild, so maybe the simplest fix is to remove the dynamic Compile Include from inside the target entirely and rely solely on the static ItemGroup with proper conditions.

Actually, I realize including non-existent files in Compile will cause compiler errors on the first pass. The better approach is to create a separate target that always runs before CoreCompile and depends on the code generation target, then dynamically adds the generated files to Compile only if they exist.

But this creates a duplication problem — if GenerateProtoCode already adds Compile items internally, then AddGeneratedProtoSources will add them again. I need to remove the Compile Include from inside GenerateProtoCode to avoid this.

For the validation target, I should batch on ProtoFile and reference all rules rather than creating a cross-product...

Building the code generation target... I'm reconsidering the Condition attribute on the ItemGroup — the transform creates new item identities, but checking if those transformed paths exist might not work as expected in a static context. I think it's cleaner to just include the generated files without the condition and trust that the GenerateProtoCode target produces them reliably.

On an incremental build, GenerateProtoCode gets skipped but AddGeneratedProtoSources still runs and adds the files that already exist from the previous build. On a fresh build, GenerateProtoCode runs first due to DependsOnTargets, creates the files, then AddGeneratedProtoSources includes them. If someone does a partial build where the files are missing, the compiler will error on the non-existent references, which is the right behavior anyway.

    <ValidationRule Include="rules\naming-conventions.rule" />

  <ItemGroup>
    <ValidationRule Include="rules\required-fields.rule" />
    <ValidationRule Include="rules\naming-conventions.rule" />

  <Target Name="GenerateProtoCode" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"

             Importance="high" />

    <WriteLinesToFile
        File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(ProtoFile.Filename).g.cs"
        Lines="// Generated from %(ProtoFile.Identity)"
        Overwrite="true" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <FileWrites Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(ProtoFile.Filename).g.cs" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  <!-- Add generated files to compilation; always runs so files survive incremental builds -->
  <Target Name="AddGeneratedProtoSources" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
      DependsOnTargets="GenerateProtoCode">
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="@(ProtoFile->'$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Filename).g.cs')" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  <!-- Validate each proto file against all rules (batch per proto, not cross-product) -->
  <Target Name="ValidateGeneratedCode" AfterTargets="GenerateProtoCode">
    <Message Text="Validating %(ProtoFile.Filename) with rules: @(ValidationRule->'%(Filename)', ', ')"
             Importance="high" />
  </Target>
</Project>

data.content:
Here's a summary of the 4 fixes:

**1. CS2002 — Duplicate Compile items** (line 16): Changed `Include` → `Update`. SDK-style projects auto-glob `**/*.cs`, so the explicit `Include="**\*.g.cs"` re-added files already globbed, causing CS2002 warnings. `Update` sets metadata on existing items without re-adding.

**2. Cross-product batching** (line 56): The original `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` + `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` in the same task caused 3×2=6 executions. Now batches only on `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` (3 runs), referencing `@(ValidationRule->'%(Filename)', ', ')` as a transform to list all rules per batch.

**3. FileWrites moved inside target** (line 42): The static `<FileWrites>` ItemGroup was evaluated at project load, not during build execution. Moved inside `GenerateProtoCode` so files are registered for clean only when actually generated.

**4. Generated files survive incremental builds** (lines 46-52): The old `<Compile Include>` inside `GenerateProtoCode` was lost when the target was skipped (incremental). Added a separate `AddGeneratedProtoSources` target with no Inputs/Outputs (always runs) and `DependsOnTargets="GenerateProtoCode"`. This also eliminated the need for the `CleanGeneratedCode` target — `FileWrites` handles clean automatically.
